page: Microsoft Azure DC48ds_v3 is a dedicated virtual machine instance belonging to the DDCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes 2458 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top 25% results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core tests, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is consistently strong across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks, though the Passmark memory score falls into the average category. Single-core performance, including stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests, remains in the average tier. Cryptographic and vector operations are supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES, contributing to strong performance in Passmark CPU encryption and extended instructions.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high memory-to-vCPU ratio provide a stable resource profile for memory-intensive workloads without the risk of resource contention. However, the lack of hyperthreading means single-threaded tasks do not see performance advantages over smaller instances, as evidenced by average single-threaded compression and text processing benchmarks. The instance is configured for database operations, redis caching, and static web serving, where it demonstrates strong throughput and transaction capabilities. It can also support moderate LLM inference tasks, though text generation speeds for larger models remain limited.
description: Microsoft Azure DC48ds_v3 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, configured with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs using an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. The instance lacks GPU resources and public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU execution, high memory bandwidth, and strong database operations, while single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database management, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC48ds_v3 is a dedicated 48-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 384 GB RAM and 2458 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC48ds_v3 is a dedicated 48-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 384 GB RAM and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server featuring high memory density, local storage, and strong multi-core processing for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated physical cores and vCPUs.
384 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Average single-core performance and no integrated GPU accelerators.
Optimized for database operations, redis caching, and memory-intensive applications.
